aliases: Beef shoulder blade steak, lean, raw

english: Beef shoulder blade steak, lean, raw

ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKN covers raw bovine meat broadly; no blade-specific lean-only sub-code exists in the public FoodEx2 catalogue. Closest parent match used.

preparation_method: trimmed of external and internal separable fat

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical raw serving of lean beef blade steak is approximately 150 g, representing a single portion before cooking, with all visible fat removed.

nutrient_source.title: Beef, chuck, blade roast, separable lean only, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 13340); cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Foodo database entry for Beef blade steak, raw, lean
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary macros (energy 113 kcal, protein 22.2 g, fat 2.6 g, carbohydrate 0.0 g) are taken directly from the Singapore HPB Foodo database for 'Beef blade steak, raw, lean'. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and micronutrient detail are cross-referenced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for beef chuck blade roast, separable lean only, raw (NDB 13340), which is the closest approved-source equivalent for the lean-only blade cut. Potassium 340 mg, calcium 4 mg, iron 1.9 mg, cholesterol 45 mg, and sodium 56 mg are taken from the HPB source. Saturated fat estimated at 1.0 g/100 g per USDA SR Legacy lean-only blade data.

gi_source.title: N/A — Beef blade steak, raw, lean contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100 g); glycemic index is not applicable
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index is not applicable for this food. Beef blade steak, raw, lean contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Pure protein and fat foods do not elicit a meaningful glycemic response and are not assigned a GI value. The numeric value 0 is used here as a sentinel to satisfy the requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric; gi_category is set to 'not_applicable' to clarify that this is not a measured GI of zero but rather an inapplicable metric. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (56 mg/100 g) and high potassium (340 mg/100 g) yield a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio consistent with DASH dietary principles
hypertension.guidance: Lean beef blade steak is suitable for hypertensive individuals when prepared without added salt, high-sodium marinades, or sauces; the naturally low sodium and high potassium content support blood pressure management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and zero sugar; high lean protein (22.2 g/100 g) with low fat (2.6 g/100 g) supports satiety and glycaemic stability without raising postprandial blood glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: Lean beef blade steak is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as a high-protein, zero-carbohydrate food; portion sizes should be moderated and sugary marinades avoided, as excessive unprocessed red meat intake has been associated with long-term insulin resistance risk in epidemiological studies.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (2.6 g/100 g) and low saturated fat (1.0 g/100 g) are favourable, but red meat consumption is associated with elevated cardiovascular risk in epidemiological literature when consumed at high frequency
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lean beef blade steak may be included in a heart-healthy diet in moderate portions (up to 2–3 times per week) when prepared without added saturated fats; individuals with established cardiovascular disease should prioritise oily fish and legumes as primary protein sources and limit red meat frequency per AHA dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat contains arachidonic acid (omega-6 fatty acid) that amplifies pro-inflammatory eicosanoid pathways relevant to RA disease activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Beef blade steak is not recommended for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its arachidonic acid content; oily fish (salmon, sardines) or skinless poultry are preferred lean protein alternatives with a more favourable omega-3 to omega-6 ratio.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beef blade steak, raw, lean is an uncooked cut of beef taken from the shoulder blade (chuck) portion of the bovine carcass. All visible external and internal separable fat has been removed, leaving lean muscle meat only. The raw meat displays a deep red colour with a fine-grained marbled texture characteristic of the blade region. Per 100 g, this lean cut provides 113 kcal, 22.2 g of high-quality complete protein, only 2.6 g of total fat (of which approximately 1.0 g is saturated fat), and 0.0 g of carbohydrates and sugar, making it one of the leanest cuts of beef available. Sodium is very low at 56 mg/100 g, while potassium is high at 340 mg/100 g, yielding a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio. Iron content is 1.9 mg/100 g (haem iron, highly bioavailable), zinc is approximately 4.5 mg/100 g, and vitamin B12 is approximately 2.1 mcg/100 g. The glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. Clinically, this cut is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes (zero carbohydrate, high protein), hypertension (low sodium, high potassium, DASH-compatible), and weight management (low calorie density). It is classified as moderate concern for cardiovascular disease due to the epidemiological association between red meat frequency and cardiovascular risk, despite the low saturated fat content of this lean preparation. It is not recommended for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to arachidonic acid content in red meat. Halal-certifiable when sourced from appropriately slaughtered cattle. Suitable for ketogenic, high-protein, and low-calorie dietary patterns. Commonly grilled, pan-seared, braised, or used in stews and stir-fries after cooking.

Secondary micronutrient values cross-referenced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 13340) for beef chuck blade roast, separable lean only, raw — the closest approved-source equivalent. Both are approved primary sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database; beef blade steak, lean only, raw contains no carbohydrates naturally as it is a pure muscle meat product. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is an animal muscle food containing no plant fibre. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae and vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: detailed fat-soluble vitamin data for this specific lean-only blade cut not individually reported in HPB Foodo; USDA SR Legacy reports negligible vitamin A and D for lean beef blade, but exact values for this preparation are not confirmed in the primary HPB source. Vitamin_e_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry. Monounsaturated_fat_g (1.1 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.1 g), trans_fat_g (0.1 g), and detailed B-vitamins (thiamine 0.07 mg, riboflavin 0.18 mg, niacin 4.5 mg, B6 0.4 mg, B12 2.1 mcg, folate 6 mcg), phosphorus (200 mg), magnesium (22 mg), zinc (4.5 mg), selenium (18 mcg), and water (74.5 g) are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 13340 (beef chuck blade roast, separable lean only, raw) as the closest approved-source equivalent; this adaptation is documented in nutrient_source. Glycemic index is set to numeric 0 as a sentinel value to satisfy the requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric; gi_category is set to 'not_applicable' to clarify that GI is inapplicable for this food, which contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. This is a Western generic food item; no Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese multilingual naming requirements apply under G6. Halal status assumes appropriate slaughter certification; consumers should verify halal certification with their supplier.
